paragraphs: The LCD Control is used to display floating-point numbers. The individual digits have the appearance of an LCD display.
The following use cases are possible with the LCD Control:
Assign a symbol to the control in the Panel Designer.
You can assign a symbol to the control
You can configure the control via the ribbon or the Properties Window.
During runtime of CANoe/CANalyzer the LCD Control indicates the current symbol value.
Here all settings are listed that you can change in the ribbon and/or in the Properties Window.
General Group
General
Name
Control Name
Here you change the name of the control. This name and the name of the panel is required for access from CAPL.
Is Visible At Runtime
Specifies whether the control is to be displayed during runtime.
tables: Here you change the name of the control. This name and the name of the panel is required for access from CAPL.
Specifies whether the control is to be displayed during runtime. CAPL Access With the function setControlVisibility, you can set, if the control is displayed during runtime or not.

Here you change the number of digits before the decimal point. Possible values: 0 – 20 If the number of digits before the decimal point exceeds the definition, the digits at the front are truncated. A triangle is displayed in the upper left corner of the element during runtime to indicate that not all digits have been displayed. If the number of digits before the decimal point is less than the definition, the extra digits are filled with zeros.

Here you change the number of decimal places. Possible values: 0 – 20 If the number of decimal places exceeds the definition, the value is truncated after the defined number. Rounding does not take place.If the number of decimal places is less than the definition, the extra spaces are filled with zeros.
Show Properties With a click on the symbol you open the Properties Window.

Here you change the background color of the LCD Control. CAPL Access With the function setControlBackColor, you can change the Background Color of the LCD Control. With the function setDefaultControlColors, you can reset the background colors to the default values defined in the Panel Designer.

Allows the control background to show through. If the property is set to True, the Background Color property is grayed out and cannot be used. If the property is set to False, the element is displayed with the specified background color.
Here you change the color for switched-off segments.
Here you change the color for segments that display the current number and are thereby switched on.
Here you change the line thickness of the segments.

Here you can enter settings relating to the size and position of the control. Note You can also resize the control by dragging its markers. Once selected, a control can be positioned anywhere on the panel. Snaplines appear on the working area to help you to line up the controls on the panel.

Note You can also resize the control by dragging its markers. Once selected, a control can be positioned anywhere on the panel. Snaplines appear on the working area to help you to line up the controls on the panel.

Note You can also resize the control by dragging its markers. Once selected, a control can be positioned anywhere on the panel. Snaplines appear on the working area to help you to line up the controls on the panel.

You can assign a symbol to the control. With a click on [...] you open the Symbol Selection dialog. Note your setting in Symbol filter. See also Configuration of the control. Valid Data Types of Symbols
Here you select the type of the symbol you want to assign to your control.

paragraphs: With the function setControlVisibility, you can set, if the control is displayed during runtime or not.
Number of Digits Group
Number of Digits
Before Decimal Point
Here you change the number of digits before the decimal point.
Possible values: 0 – 20

If the number of digits before the decimal point exceeds the definition, the digits at the front are truncated. A triangle is displayed in the upper left corner of the element during runtime to indicate that not all digits have been displayed. If the number of digits before the decimal point is less than the definition, the extra digits are filled with zeros.
After Decimal Point
Here you change the number of decimal places.

If the number of decimal places exceeds the definition, the value is truncated after the defined number. Rounding does not take place.If the number of decimal places is less than the definition, the extra spaces are filled with zeros.
More Group
Show Properties
With a click on the symbol you open the Properties Window.
Group: —
Appearance
Background Color
Here you change the background color of the LCD Control.
Background Is Transparent
Allows the control background to show through.
If the property is set to True, the Background Color property is grayed out and cannot be used.
If the property is set to False, the element is displayed with the specified background color.
Segment Color Off
Here you change the color for switched-off segments.
Segment Color On
Here you change the color for segments that display the current number and are thereby switched on.
Segment Strength
Here you change the line thickness of the segments.
Group: —
Layout
Location/Size
Here you can enter settings relating to the size and position of the control.
Note
You can also resize the control by dragging its markers.
Once selected, a control can be positioned anywhere on the panel. Snaplines appear on the working area to help you to line up the controls on the panel.
Symbol
You can assign a symbol to the control.
With a click on [...] you open the Symbol Selection dialog. Note your setting in Symbol filter.
See also Configuration of the control.
Valid Data Types of Symbols
Symbol Filter
Here you select the type of the symbol you want to assign to your control.
Assigning Controls
tables: Note You can also resize the control by dragging its markers. Once selected, a control can be positioned anywhere on the panel. Snaplines appear on the working area to help you to line up the controls on the panel.
